vulns.title: Oracle PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools Missing Authentication for Critical Function Vulnerability
vulns.summary: Oracle PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools contains a missing authentication for critical function vulnerability which could allow an unauthenticated attacker to obtain takeover of PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools.
vulns.technicalSummary: This vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code on affected installations of Oracle PeopleSoft. Although authentication is required to exploit this vulnerability, the existing authentication mechanism can be bypassed. The ZDI has.
vulns.breakdown: This vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code on affected installations of Oracle PeopleSoft. Although authentication is required to exploit this vulnerability, the existing authentication mechanism can be bypassed. The ZDI has.
vulns.whatBroke: Oracle PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools contains a missing authentication for critical function vulnerability which could allow an unauthenticated attacker to obtain takeover of PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools.
vulns.reality: Realistic when the affected product is exposed and unpatched. The updater only includes this because public tooling signal was found at 12hrformat/CVE-2026-35273-POC.
vulns.exploitSyntax:
# authorized lab / owned targets only
git clone https://github.com/12hrformat/CVE-2026-35273-POC
cd CVE-2026-35273-POC
python3 <script>.py --target https://<authorized-target>
# confirm exact flags in the linked GitHub README before validation
chains: initial access through exposed PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools
internal pivot from an internet-facing control point
lateral movement if secrets or management access are recovered
vulns.weaponized: Likely worth tracking because public tooling exists and recent source activity was observed.
